content.text2: A real-time parental control platform built for peace of mind
content.text3: Sentinel Kids is a child safety monitoring platform that enables parents to track and control their children's app usage and content in real time. I led the front-end development at Samarix, building the platform from the ground up using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion — focused on delivering an experience that felt approachable for non-technical parents while handling complex real-time data under the hood.

content.text2: Safety tools that feel like surveillance
content.text3: Parental control software has a reputation for being clunky, overwhelming, and anxiety-inducing. The core challenge was designing a dashboard that surfaces critical information — live activity feeds, app usage stats, content flags — without making parents feel like they are operating a CCTV control room. Every screen needed to feel calm, clear, and actionable.

content.caption: The main dashboard surfaces the most important activity at a glance

content.text3: The platform serves two distinct user types: parents managing permissions, and children using monitored devices. I designed and built role-based access controls that cleanly separate these experiences — parents get a full activity and approval interface, while the child-facing layer stays lightweight. Notification systems were built to alert parents in real time without creating constant noise.

content.caption: Role-based views ensure each user sees only what is relevant to them
App approval and blocking flows designed to be resolved in under 10 seconds

content.text3: I integrated Framer Motion throughout the platform to deliver smooth page transitions and micro-animations. In a product built around safety and monitoring, smooth, confident motion signals reliability. Abrupt or janky transitions would undermine user trust — so every state change, panel open, and data update was animated deliberately.

content.caption: Animated state transitions keep the interface feeling responsive and alive
Live activity feed with categorised content alerts

content.text3: Beyond the core dashboard, I built out notification management, content category controls, and device-level settings. Accessibility was a key focus throughout — the platform needed to work for parents of all technical backgrounds, across all screen sizes.

content.caption: Content category controls — parents can allow or restrict by type
Mobile-responsive layout — fully functional on any screen

content.text2: Building for people, not just parents
content.text3: Sentinel Kids reinforced how much UX clarity matters when the stakes are emotional. Parents using this platform are worried. The interface's job is to reduce that worry — not amplify it. Optimising Lighthouse scores and implementing code splitting alongside this kept the experience fast, which matters especially for users checking in under stress.
